finding_results.notes: Replaced the recap-heavy wall arithmetic ending with a new beat: Kem visibly covers the signet, keeps his updated count, and Vel sets a concrete spend rule for tomorrow before he refuses the Light Charm.
Compressed the Vault callback to a single-sentence reference and let the settlement soundscape do the contrastive work.
Aligned the Aldraste crest description with the continuity sheet's canonical closed-circle symbol.
Added an opening interval anchor that states both the pursuit's elapsed travel time and Kem's elapsed time out of the Vault within the first lines.

Put the pursuit back on-page through settlement gossip, made Dren verify that the Hoarded are closing from Ashmarker, and changed the group's departure plan to an off-road before-dawn escape with no visible abilities.

Changed the chapter's back half from simple provisioning to a danger-state shift: Dren sells at a loss, ditches cart visibility, and reroutes the southbound departure because the pursuit is close enough to monetize.
Chapter now runs 4516 words while preserving the heading contract.

finding_results.notes: Six targeted edits across the Dren negotiation scene. (1) Route description roughened with casual profanity ('Southern routes are shit') and shorter fragmented syntax. (2) Money reaction tightened: Dren responds with 'That's a damn double eastern run — before I lose half of it dumping the cart,' integrating profanity as connective tissue and folding the cart-loss concern into dialogue instead of narrator summary. (3) Terrain dangers shortened and roughened with physical gesture (spitting) and punchier phrasing. (4) 'How long' exchange compressed. (5) Mira disclosure broken from a single composed block into fragments surfacing through the negotiation: first an oblique reference to 'a healer up in Roth I'm trying to get to, and every run's part of that,' then the truncated personal detail 'My girl. Mira. Her legs are —' with a self-interruption and practical redirect ('Point is, your number gets me closer'). Dren no longer delivers Mira's name, age, illness, healer cost, and motive in one tidy speech. (6) Terms negotiation broken from a single block into shorter transactional pieces ('Half up front. Other half before I turn back.'). The revised exchange now contains at least three unmistakably rough trader lines ('Southern routes are shit,' 'That's a damn double eastern run,' 'South routes pay well because south routes are shit'), and no single speech cleanly packages route danger, Mira's illness, and his motive. Healer location updated to 'Roth' per continuity sheet. All existing productive roughness preserved elsewhere in the scene (the chair joke, 'pale bastard,' 'bastard standing next to him,' 'Wake me if somebody's dying').

finding_results.notes: Replaced repeated type-label commentary with concrete physical observation and tighter Kem-specific inference across the arrival, healer, tavern, signet reaction, market, and Dren negotiation beats while preserving the chapter's factual content and state shift.

pass_summaries.summary: Anchored the chase timeline at the top of the chapter, corrected the Aldraste crest continuity and the overexplained Vault-silence callback, turned Dren's hiring into an immediate pursuit-driven reroute with visible economic cost, and rebuilt the camp ending around a concrete new travel rule and Kem's deliberate no-Light choice.

Revised Dren's negotiation exchange across six edit points to replace composed expository mini-speeches with rougher haggling texture. Mira's disclosure is now fragmented across the negotiation rather than delivered in one explanatory block. Casual profanity added as connective tissue per Dren's style-bible profile. Terms negotiation broken into shorter transactional pieces. No structural changes to the chapter; all other scenes, the heading contract, and continuity details preserved.

Chapter 7's prose pass removed the recurring explanatory-classifier tic and sharpened observation at the road, settlement, tavern, keeper, and market-yard beats without changing continuity, chapter function, or dialogue texture.
